subject: Human Resources
title: Employee Monitoring and Surveillance
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Employee Monitoring and Surveillance.

hook.text: Your employer can read your work emails, track your keystrokes, and monitor your screen—all legally, in most states.

buildup.text: Employers have broad rights to monitor company-owned devices, email, internet use, and even GPS in work vehicles.

discovery.text: The Electronic Communications Privacy Act generally allows employer monitoring on company systems—especially with notice.

twist.text: Remote work expanded monitoring—productivity trackers, screenshot capture, and mouse movement detection became commonplace.

climax.text: Excessive surveillance erodes trust and engagement. Companies must balance legitimate oversight with employee dignity.

punchline.text: Monitoring is legal. But just because you can doesn't mean you should.

quiz.question: What is generally legal regarding employer monitoring?
options: Monitoring company-owned devices and systems with notice
